page: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DLDSv5 server family. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this server features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores using Hyper-V virtualization. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It is equipped with 3686 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with top-tier performance in the Passmark CPU encryption test and strong results in multi-core stress-ng tests. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI, which accelerate workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, registering strong read and write speeds for both small and large blocks. However, single-core performance and text generation for larger language models remain in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96lds_v5 offers high compute density and substantial local storage without the premium cost of high-memory configurations. The reduced memory footprint makes it a resource-efficient choice for compute-heavy tasks that do not require massive RAM arrays. This server is highly suitable for multi-threaded batch processing, software compilation, web serving, and database operations that leverage its fast local disk and strong multi-core throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the DLDSv5 family, featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, representing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 3686 GB of local storage. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers top-tier performance in cryptographic encryption and strong multi-core processing, software compilation, and database operations. While single-core performance is average, its high compute density and integrated local storage offer qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive workloads. The D96lds_v5 is best suited for multi-threaded batch processing, web serving, and database applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3686 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance and top-tier cryptographic encryption capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3686 GB local storage for high-density workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute server featuring ninety-six dedicated virtual cores, integrated local storage, and optimized memory allocation for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
192 GB of RAM providing a 2.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio
3686 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier cryptographic encryption and strong multi-core benchmark performance
Optimized for multi-threaded batch processing, software compilation, and databases
